The router injects into handler structs like any other dependency.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router => ROUTERDIR
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"
)

type Docs struct {
	Router *router.Router
}

//fabrik:http GET /routes
func (d *Docs) List(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	_ = d.Router.Routes()
}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"net/http"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() error {
	// Providers
	r := router.New()
	webDocs := &web.Docs{
		Router: r,
	}

	// Register
	r.Method("GET", "/routes", webDocs.List)

	// Serve
	addr := ":8080"
	if p := os.Getenv("PORT"); p != "" {
		addr = ":" + p
	}
	return http.ListenAndServe(addr, r)
}
